title: Single-cell RNA sequencing enabling individual disease treatment
author: Ari Turunen
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The revolutionary single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) measures the activity of all genes separately in each cell, giving a more accurate picture of how cells differ from each other. This technology will produce a vast amount of information on diseases, such as cancer. There are millions of cancer cells in a tumour. Data analysis requires increasingly more computing capacity and efficient algorithms as the number of cells and samples analysed increases.
